architecture.summary: The paper evaluates verifier-guided search (beam search with step-level candidate ranking) for mathematical reasoning in LLMs. A generator produces candidate partial solution paths at each step; a verifier (OVM or PRM) scores candidates; top-k paths advance to the next step. The system compares this against repeated sampling baselines. The paper is empirical/analytical: it measures coverage (fraction of problems with ≥1 correct solution) as sample size and candidate count scale, identifies root causes of performance degradation, and tests two inference-time modifications (stochastic selection, Monte Carlo rollout) to reduce verifier reliance.

biblio.title: Scaling Flaws of Verifier-Guided Search in Mathematical Reasoning

claims.statement: PRM-guided search based on DeepSeekMath achieves over 20% higher performance than repeated sampling when sample size is 1, but by sample size of 16, verifier-guided search becomes inferior to repeated sampling, reaching approximately 5% lower performance when scaled to 32
OVM-guided search performance degradation on GSM8K is approximately 10%, increasing to around 20% on MATH (measured at sample size 32 vs. sample size 1)
Scaling flaws are more severe on OOD problems: OVM-guided search performance degradation increases from 19.3% on in-distribution Level 4 to 25.8% in OOD-L4 setting at sample size 32
A large proportion of OVM-guided search failures occur during the selection stage: 88.6% on GSM8K (Mistral), 80.0% on MATH (DeepSeekMath), 84.3% on OOD-L4, and 81.2% on OOD-L5
Stochastic selection with temperature 1 improves selection stage accuracy over OVM deterministic selection by up to 11.2% on OOD-L4 and OOD-L5

One-time Monte Carlo rollout with λ=1 (full reliance on simulated reward) yields the highest accuracy gain, underscoring that relying entirely on rollout reward without verifier-predicted score outperforms verifier evaluation
Increasing the number of generated candidates fails to improve and even degrades the performance of verifier-guided search

Verifier-guided search is designed to improve performance on difficult problems, yet these challenging scenarios are more susceptible to verifier failures, exacerbating scaling flaws and contradicting the intended purpose
